scene.description: The origin layer. You try to remember becoming a Keeper and four memories answer at once, each whole, each true. The system won't let any one of them be the first.

fragments.label: Origin — You Chose This
fragments.text: You became a Keeper willingly. You remember the decision, the weight of it, wanting it. It feels like the real one. They all do.

fragments.label: Origin — You Were Assigned
fragments.text: You were assigned. No choice, just a posting and a badge. This memory is just as detailed as the one where you chose.

fragments.label: Origin — You Replaced Someone
fragments.text: There was a Keeper before you at this desk. You took their place. You can't tell if you remember them or remember being them.

fragments.label: Origin — There Is None
fragments.symbol: A fourth memory of having no origin at all. You were simply always here, with no before. It contradicts the other three and is no less convincing.

fragments.label: Maya Was In All of Them
fragments.text: Whichever origin you reach for, Maya is already in it — greeting you, assigning you, replacing the one before. She is the only constant across every beginning. She is more your origin than any of these are.

fragments.label: The One Who Watches You Choose
fragments.symbol: At the edge of all four origins stands a fifth figure, not choosing, only watching which origin you'll pick. It has your posture. It is the version of you that comes after this one.

interpretations.label: Choose the willing origin as real
Choose the assigned origin as real
Accept there is no original — only versions
